title: PR-1 real Worker and default Web evidence
summary: Independently prove the repaired GameSession in a real browser Worker for at least 600 wall-clock seconds and on the default Web route, including cadence, backpressure and moving/working publication evidence.
acceptance: A real Chromium module Worker, not an in-process HarnessBrowserWorker or fake timer, stays alive at requested speed 1 for at least 600000 browser-monotonic wall-clock ms after Ready and advances from tick 0 by at least 18000 authoritative ticks

The 600-second run is passive after InitSession: no UI advance, wait, drain, command-drain, direct runScheduledQuantum or repeated command loop drives time; the report records start/end wall time and tick, minute checkpoints, publication count and fatal/closed count

Cadence evidence has strictly increasing coherent publication ticks, at least one advancing publication in every 60-second checkpoint window and no gap longer than 5000 ms; metrics evidence records droppedSnapshots and queuedReliableMessages, and focused forced-backpressure evidence confirms latest-wins snapshots with ordered reliable delivery

Under the unchanged 100 ms / 3-tick quantum, real Worker coherent projection pairs show moving in at least two consecutive pairs and working in at least two later consecutive pairs before terminal idle, with matching sequence, tick, world hash and read-model hash basis

The default Web route at 1424 x 861 passively renders separate moving and working frames from projectionSource=game-session-worker, keeps at least eight residents/resources/details on the same session basis and mechanically excludes fixture, UI advance, wait and drain gameplay authority
Fixed-seed Worker/headless parity, schema-v3 fail-closed behavior, 18000/100000-tick conservation and prior WM-0166 gates are rerun without changing production code or lowering thresholds

The report and compact artifact include raw aggregate evidence sufficient for independent review: elapsed ms, initial/final tick, per-minute ticks, publication/lifecycle sequence, maximum publication gap, dropped snapshots, maximum reliable queue depth, fatal count and exact commands/results
No product implementation, protocol/schema/projection/save change, public compatibility promise, new runtime dependency, PR-2+ feature or release action

history.details: Scoped as independent QA-only real Worker 600-second and default Web evidence after WM-0167. Added exact cadence, backpressure, lifecycle-publication and no-UI-clock gates; task remains proposed until WM-0167 is done.
Real Chromium Worker PASS: 600010.5ms, tick 0->18000, 10 minute checkpoints, 6001 publications, max gap 127.1ms, moving/working x2, fatal/closed 0; Web 13/13, Worker 19/19, quality 462/462

changes_requested: High: the default-Web moving/working render gate is not proven. apps/web/src/web-shell.e2e.test.ts waits on __wm0168WorkerInbound raw UiDelta messages captured beside the product subscription; WebShellDebugPayload exposes no resident activity, so a broken Web adapter/render path that maps every activity to idle would still pass. Add product-consumed/rendered moving and working observations on the same coherent GameSession basis at 1424x861. Medium: the report says the order-sensitive Worker.prototype.addEventListener wrapper was replaced by a constructor-only read-only proxy, but the test still monkey-patches Worker.prototype.addEventListener, changing listener/removal semantics and duplicating capture; remove the prototype patch or document and prove semantic equivalence. Medium: real-run closedCount is hardcoded to 0 rather than observed; record actual browser-session/Worker lifecycle closure evidence. All required checks otherwise pass, including independently rerun real Chromium Worker 19/19 with 600101.9ms, tick 0->18000, 10 advancing checkpoints, max gap 126ms, Web 13/13, focused 44/44, 18k/100k zero leaks, and quality 462/462.

saveLoadStance: Evidence-only. No GameSession save mutation or public compatibility; unsupported load remains fail-closed.
forbiddenPaths: packages/sim-core/**, packages/sim-worker production files, packages/sim-protocol/** and apps/web production files
mock Worker, in-process HarnessBrowserWorker, fake timer, explicit runScheduledQuantum, advance, wait, drain or command-drain evidence substituted for the real 600-second gate
reducing 600000 ms, 18000 ticks, 5000 ms maximum gap, 3-tick quantum or two-consecutive-publication lifecycle requirements
changing implementation to make evidence pass; product failures must block back to simulation-engineer with structured evidence
fixture/scenario-runner authority, protocol/schema/projection/save changes, new dependencies, PR-2+ scope or release actions
executionLane: qa-performance only after WM-0167 done; final review remains independent reviewer
singleWriterRule: WM-0168 is the sole evidence writer only after WM-0167 closes. WM-0166 stays blocked until WM-0168 is independently verified, integrated and done.

review.summary: High: the default-Web moving/working render gate is not proven. apps/web/src/web-shell.e2e.test.ts waits on __wm0168WorkerInbound raw UiDelta messages captured beside the product subscription; WebShellDebugPayload exposes no resident activity, so a broken Web adapter/render path that maps every activity to idle would still pass. Add product-consumed/rendered moving and working observations on the same coherent GameSession basis at 1424x861. Medium: the report says the order-sensitive Worker.prototype.addEventListener wrapper was replaced by a constructor-only read-only proxy, but the test still monkey-patches Worker.prototype.addEventListener, changing listener/removal semantics and duplicating capture; remove the prototype patch or document and prove semantic equivalence. Medium: real-run closedCount is hardcoded to 0 rather than observed; record actual browser-session/Worker lifecycle closure evidence. All required checks otherwise pass, including independently rerun real Chromium Worker 19/19 with 600101.9ms, tick 0->18000, 10 advancing checkpoints, max gap 126ms, Web 13/13, focused 44/44, 18k/100k zero leaks, and quality 462/462.
